teaser.title: Is It Possible to Leave the Healthcare System to the Private Sector?

about.heading: Ayberk Ceyran
about.paragraph1: I was born in 2007. I'm interested in economics, software, data analysis, and artificial intelligence, and I keep working to develop myself in these areas — particularly in Python, web development, and data science. I'm also preparing for the SAT and aiming to study economics in Canada.
about.paragraph2: I created this website to share my projects, research, articles, and the work I'll keep developing over time. While documenting my own learning process, I hope the content I produce can also be useful to others.
about.paragraph3: In the long run, I aim to develop projects that bring technology and economics together, pursue academic work, and contribute to the production of knowledge.
about.paragraph4: The most important figure who has inspired me throughout my life is Gazi Mustafa Kemal Atatürk, the founder of our Republic. The importance he placed on science, reason, freedom, and modernization is among the core values I hold as well.
about.paragraph5: Thank you for visiting my website.

items.title: Open Budget Initiative
items.description: A project making public spending visible to everyone through plain-language, regular reports.

items.title: Neighborhood Councils
items.description: A network of regular meetings where decisions are made in the neighborhood, not behind a desk, and residents have a direct voice.

items.title: Youth Policy Workshop
items.description: An education and mentorship program aiming to make young people founding partners in politics, not just spectators.

posts.title: Is It Possible to Leave the Healthcare System to the Private Sector?
posts.description: A comparison of private and public healthcare models — from the US and Cuba to Taiwan's hybrid system, ranked #1 in the world for seven years running.
